title: How Can Muslims Help America? l President Hamza Yusuf l Zaytuna College
description:
American Muslims have long struggled to integrate these two seemingly polarized aspects of their identities. At Zaytuna College, they discover that they need not experience identity crises. They deepen their understanding of the ways these two aspects of their identity are compatible. They discover that Islam belongs in the west — having first arrived on American soil during the slave trade — and has an important role to play in America’s realization of its highest ideals. 

In honor of thirty years of Zaytuna College this year, we share this clip from the 1990s, President Hamza Yusuf reflects on the important role Muslims play in helping America realize its highest ideals.  
 
Zaytuna College, the first accredited Muslim liberal arts college in the United States, offers a rigorous liberal arts education grounded in the great works of the Islamic and Euro-American traditions. We aim to prepare graduates for ethical leadership and meaningful contribution to the greater good.  
 
Learn about our BA & MA programs:  https://zaytuna.edu/academics 
 
Support the revival of the Islamic intellectual and ethical tradition and the cultivation of tomorrow’s Muslim leaders: https://zaytuna.edu/cultivate

title: More Than a Degree: How Zaytuna Changed Everything | Graduating Senior Reflects
description:
What happens when your college education nourishes both your mind and your soul?

In this clip, 2026 graduating senior Abdel reflects on his four years at Zaytuna College, America's first accredited Muslim liberal arts college, and what it truly meant to be surrounded by likeminded peers and scholars who pushed him to think deeper, ask harder questions, and grow closer to God in the process.

From studying the great texts of the Western and Islamic traditions to building a community rooted in shared values and sincere pursuit of knowledge, his journey is a testament to what education looks like when it doesn't separate the intellectual from the spiritual.

Is a Zaytuna right for you or someone you know? Explore our programs: https://zaytuna.edu/academics

Zaytuna College is located in Berkeley, California and offers a BA in Liberal Arts and Islamic Studies and an MA in Islamic Texts.

title: Sacred Stewardship and Service: Learning Permaculture at Zaytuna College
description:
A graduate of the Permaculture Design Certificate (PDC) course at Zaytuna College describes how this program prepares her for better stewardship and how she plans to translate knowledge into service.   
 
Zaytuna College’s biannual Permaculture Design Certificate course (PDC) is a transformative program that cultivates greater alignment with nature, community, and oneself. The two-week interdisciplinary program draws students from all over the world and integrates ecological design, traditional knowledge, and spiritual stewardship.

title: Where Is Our Community? l President Hamza Yusuf l Zaytuna College
description:
In this khutba delivered at Zaytuna College during Dhu’l Hijjah in June 2026, President Hamza Yusuf shares his reflections on the state of the Muslim community, the weight of the trust (amana) we have been given, the challenges we face as a community, and what Muslims, as ambassadors of Islam, most need to embody in the world today.

title: What Two Words Fill Everything in the Cosmos? l Shaykh Faraz Khan l  Zaytuna College
description:
About two of the most familiar words to Muslims — subhanallah and alhamdulillah — there is much commentary.  In this clip, Shaykh Faraz Khan of Zaytuna College explains these interpretations.  When they are spoken by those who deeply realize that all praise belongs to God, in all situations — those whose patience has been transformed into contentment — do they fill the entire cosmos. 

Click to watch the full session: 
https://youtu.be/INaHEJLEGKc?si=ZmtVgIeUmODz1-bM

Make a gift today: https://zaytuna.edu/cultivate  
We are pleased to share weekly clips from a special course from the Zaytuna archive: Shaykh Faraz Khan’s exegesis of Sura al-Kahf. Taught as part of the Zaytuna Honors program across twenty sessions in 2013, in a crowded room of students and seekers on our first Berkeley campus building. This course was open to the public due to our longstanding commitment to community education. ────────────────────────────────────────

title: What Fuels Our Journey to God?  l Shaykh Faraz Khan l  Zaytuna College
description:
Repentance (tawba) is an obligation for believers. It also propels our journey to God. What are the conditions for fulfilling this obligation? How do sincerity, remorse, resolve, or repeated sins relate to repentance? In this clip, Shaykh Faraz Khan explains how to fulfill this obligation and ensure we never run out of fuel and get stuck on our spiritual journey.

title: How Much Entertainment Is Too Much?  l Shaykh Faraz Khan l  Zaytuna College
description:
The nature of this world is that it is always leaving us; permanence is only found in the afterlife.  Our work here is to plant seeds, do good work, spread light — and yes, to a certain extent, take enjoyment in this world. In this clip, Sharykh Faraz Khan of Zaytuna College explores entertainment in balance, as an aspect of our humanity, and entertainment that consumes our lives and leads to social decay.

title: Shaykh Hamza Yusuf on AI, Reading, and Human Wisdom | Book Club Selections for Muharram 2026
description:
This Muharram, the First Command Book Club reads two important works that explore the promises and challenges of artificial intelligence: "The AI Mirror: How to Reclaim Our Humanity in an Age of Machine Thinking" by Shannon Vallor, and "Magnifica Humanitas," an encyclical letter by Pope Leo XIV. 

Encyclical letters address matters of significance to the wider human community. Throughout history, pontiffs wrote encyclicals on subjects ranging from labor and social justice to environmental stewardship. As communities across the world grapple with the implications of artificial intelligence, this recent encyclical offers an opportunity to consider how one major religious tradition responds to these questions. Muslims, too, must think carefully about the ethical and spiritual challenges posed by emerging technologies and contribute meaningful guidance to the conversation. 

At Zaytuna College, we strive to preserve and cultivate natural intelligence. Reading remains one of the most important means by which we do so—and God willing, by which we rise. 

Join us as we embark on the sixth year of the First Command Book Club. We hope to see you there. 

Learn More about the First Command Book Club and Join: https://bookclub.zaytuna.edu/ 

The First Command Book Club is a member benefit of 12000 Strong. To join, simply make a monthly gift to Zaytuna College in any amount. Membership includes access to all previous sessions, reading guides for every book, a 12% discount at the Zaytuna Bookstore, and monthly live sessions with President Hamza Yusuf and special guests.

Learn about 12000 Strong: https://zaytuna.edu/give/12000-strong

Purchase these books from the Zaytuna College Bookstore: https://bookstore.zaytuna.edu/

Subscribe to Zaytuna College's channel: https://www.youtube.com/Zaytuna

title: What Is The Essence of Revelation?  l Shaykh Faraz Khan l  Zaytuna College
description:
Even more than His very existence, God’s revelation emphasizes Oneness. In this clip, Shaykh Faraz Khan of Zaytuna College considers the implications of absolute monotheism. However confused or distracted we might become by other forms of apparent power, true power is  always One. However scattered our hopes, aspirations, and aims may be, as believers we direct our hearts solely to our Creator.
Click to watch the full session: 
https://youtu.be/ZcUD_g0nRuw?si=BsJZFVX65Q8rc_qj

title: Formal Logic II: Judgement and Reasoning | Zaytuna College Online Course
description:
This online course — the second taught by Dr. Phillbert Cheng in a two–part series offered by Zaytuna College — continues the study of formal logic by exploring judgement, propositions, and reasoning. 

Students in this course will learn how thoughts are structured, claims are tested, and arguments are formed.  This course offers a sample of Zaytuna College’s singular liberal arts curriculum comprising the great works of the Islamic and Euro-American traditions. 

The purpose of a classic liberal arts education is to provide the foundational tools for lifelong learning and to train students to think clearly, communicate persuasively, and contribute meaningfully to the greater good.  For many, logic was considered the jewel of the trivium — grammar, logic, and rhetoric — as it cultivated the essence of what it is to be human: the ability to reason. 

All of Zaytuna's online courses are available to stream on demand and available to take at your own pace. 

Sign up today and explore Formal Logic II with Dr. Phillbert Cheng: https://onlinecourses.zaytuna.edu/courses/formal-logic-2 

Register for Introduction to Logic: https://onlinecourses.zaytuna.edu/courses/introduction-to-logic

Register for both Logic courses at a discounted price: https://onlinecourses.zaytuna.edu/bundles/formal-logic-bundle

Browse all online courses: https://onlinecourses.zaytuna.edu/

Learn about our Bachelor's and Master's programs: https://zaytuna.edu/academics

title: Where Does Human Effort Meet Divine Mercy?  l Shaykh Faraz Khan l  Zaytuna College
description:
Faith in God does not entail worldly detachment; believers must fully participate in the labor of serving creation. Our human effort is required — and believers must also recognize that any outcome of our effort, without exception, is divine mercy.  In this commentary, Sharykh Faraz cautions against a detachment that leads to social breakdown and emphasizes that both our toil and its outcomes are from God.
Click to watch the full session: 
https://youtu.be/JRJ7zO-ussQ?si=W4m46nMlKUk5QJQb

title: What Distinguishes a Devoted Servant? l Shaykh Faraz Khan l  Zaytuna College
description:
To understand the qualities of a devoted servant is to grasp how we too might become more pleasing to God.  In this commentary, Shaykh Faraz  examines the qualities of ubudiyyah, or servitude — and what distinguishes the  devoted servants of God — to help us understand how we might open our own worldly means.

title: The Histories by Herodotus | First Command Book Club June 2026
description:
The First Command Book Club's June 2026 book is "The Histories" by Herodotus. 

On Sunday, June 14, the First Command Book Club will discuss "The Histories" by Herodotus, widely regarded as the founding text of Western history and one of the earliest accounts of the rise of the Persian Empire. Hosted by President Hamza Yusuf and featuring historian and Islamic scholar Dr. Khalid Blankinship, this discussion is exclusively for 12000 Strong.
 
12000 Strong is our growing global community of steadfast supporters whose modest monthly contributions add up to an immense impact at Zaytuna College. Join today with a monthly pledge of any amount to invest in a better future and embark on your own learning journey.

God willing, your monthly investment in the revival of the Islamic intellectual and ethical tradition will live on as sadaqah jariyah — perpetual charity — as the light of knowledge and faith is passed on for generations. 

Join 12000 Strong today: https://zaytuna.edu/give/12000-strong

Learn more about the First Command Book Club: https://bookclub.zaytuna.edu/
